purpose: Valibot is a modular, type-safe schema library for TypeScript used to validate unknown data (server input, forms, config files) at runtime with static type inference. Its modular API design enables tree shaking for minimal bundle sizes (starting under 700 bytes).

public_api.name: object
public_api.summary: Creates an object schema from a map of property name to schema.

public_api.name: string
public_api.summary: Schema that validates string values.

public_api.name: number
public_api.summary: Schema that validates number values.

public_api.name: boolean
public_api.summary: Schema that validates boolean values.

public_api.name: array
public_api.summary: Schema that validates arrays of a given item schema.

public_api.name: pipe
public_api.summary: Composes a schema with validation/transformation actions (e.g. email(), minLength()).

public_api.name: email
public_api.summary: Pipe action validating that a string is an email.

public_api.name: minLength
public_api.summary: Pipe action validating minimum string/array length.

public_api.name: parse
public_api.summary: Parses input against schema; throws ValiError on validation failure.

public_api.name: safeParse
public_api.summary: Non-throwing parse; returns a result object with success flag and issues or output.

public_api.name: is
public_api.summary: Type guard function returning whether the input matches the schema.

public_api.name: InferOutput
public_api.summary: Utility type that infers the TypeScript output type of a schema.

public_api.name: InferInput
public_api.summary: Utility type that infers the TypeScript input type of a schema.

public_api.name: optional
public_api.summary: Wraps a schema to also accept undefined.

public_api.name: nullable
public_api.summary: Wraps a schema to also accept null.

public_api.name: union
public_api.summary: Schema that validates against one of several option schemas.

public_api.name: variant
public_api.summary: Discriminated union schema based on a key field.

public_api.name: intersect
public_api.summary: Schema representing intersection of multiple schemas.

public_api.name: picklist
public_api.summary: Schema that validates one of a fixed set of literal values.

public_api.name: literal
public_api.summary: Schema matching a specific literal value.

public_api.name: transform
public_api.summary: Pipe action that transforms the output value.

public_api.name: check
public_api.summary: Pipe action that runs a custom boolean validation.

public_api.name: ValiError
public_api.summary: Error thrown by parse() containing validation issues.

public_api.name: flatten
public_api.summary: Flattens issues into a nested object keyed by field path.

public_api.name: parser
public_api.summary: Returns a reusable parse function bound to the schema.

public_api.name: safeParser
public_api.summary: Returns a reusable safeParse function bound to the schema.

usage_patterns.title: Define and parse a schema
usage_patterns.body: Import everything as a namespace (`import * as v from 'valibot'`), build a schema with v.object / v.string / v.pipe, then call v.parse(schema, input). parse throws ValiError on failure; use v.safeParse for non-throwing result, or v.is for a type guard.

usage_patterns.title: Compose validations via pipe
usage_patterns.body: Attach validation and transformation actions to a base schema with v.pipe, e.g. v.pipe(v.string(), v.email(), v.minLength(8)). This modular approach enables tree shaking so unused actions are dropped from the bundle.

usage_patterns.title: Infer TypeScript types from schemas
usage_patterns.body: Use `type T = v.InferOutput<typeof Schema>` (and InferInput) to derive static types directly from runtime schemas, keeping one source of truth.

usage_patterns.title: Async validation
usage_patterns.body: Valibot provides parallel *Async variants (objectAsync, arrayAsync, pipeAsync, parseAsync, safeParseAsync, checkAsync, transformAsync, etc.) for schemas that require asynchronous checks or transformations.

gotchas: parse() throws ValiError on invalid input; prefer safeParse() when you do not want exceptions.
Actions like email(), minLength(), and transform() are not methods on a schema — they must be combined via v.pipe(schema, ...actions).
Async schemas/actions (e.g. objectAsync, pipeAsync) require the matching parseAsync/safeParseAsync; sync parse will not await them.
Object schemas come in variants: object (strip unknown — default), strictObject (reject unknown keys), looseObject (preserve unknown keys), and objectWithRest.
Valibot's API is modular: always import what you use (e.g. `import * as v from 'valibot'`) so tree shaking actually reduces bundle size.

examples.title: Login schema with parse and type inference
